items.summary: General Contribution Day 1
Opening Ceremony + Women in Drupal Award
items.description: Join the Opening Ceremony for an exciting start to DrupalCon Barcelona 2024. Right after the welcome coffee, the Organizing Secretariat, Advisory Board members, and Drupal Association staff are eager to welcome you to the vibrant city of Barcelona.

items.summary: Lunch Break, Tuesday
DRIESNOTE AUDITORIUM
items.description: Belgium-born Drupal founder Dries Buytaert is a pioneer in the Open Source web publishing and digital experience platform space. For his customary (and 40th ever) #Driesnote presentation in Barcelona, Dries will give an update on the State of Drupal.

items.summary: Group Photo
Delayed but Not Doomed: Ensuring Security for Your Drupal 7 Site After End of Life (Sponsored by HeroDevs)
items.description: Drupal 7 is reaching end of life, but extended security support from HeroDevs means you can stay secure and compliant until you’re ready to migrate.

items.summary: Designing for Low Literacy: Enhancing Accessibility and Understanding
items.description: Designing for low literacy: Enhancing accessibility and understanding through techniques such as visual storytelling, iconography, and simplified language, to communicate information effectively to users with limited reading skills.

items.summary: Drupal at the core of Schwabe Group’s Digital Strategy
items.description: Our CEO will present together with the Director Global Digital Marketing at Schwabe Group the digital transformation and decision process behind the selection of Drupal as an essential solution component in their digital strategy.

items.summary: Gander in practice: start monitoring your site’s performance today
items.description: At this workshop we will teach attendees how to use Gander, Drupal’s new performance testing framework. We will teach how to write performance tests and how to improve existing PHPUnit tests with performance related assertions.

items.summary: How combining open source platforms can make your business and the community stronger. An update on Mautic, Drupal and the open DXP.
items.description: Bridge the gap between two powerful platforms, Drupal and Mautic, within the context of an open Digital Experience Platform (DXP).

items.summary: Movement and Music - Wellness
Networking in the Solidarity Economy: Cooperatives, Sociocracy, and Alternative Work Models for a Sustainable Future
items.description: Are you part of an organization that embraces cooperative structures, sociocracy, or other alternative work models?

items.summary: Over 15000 courses and almost 100 different providers - Developing and maintaining a custom Course Marketing system
items.description: Everyone is special. No more so than the almost 1000 further education providers across Ireland who provide information for use on the National Further Education Portal.

items.summary: Running a fleet of web sites with ease via LocalGov Drupal Microsites Platform
items.description: Large organisations like councils, governments, universities have always needed to run a varying number of small websites. However, these are often on disparate technologies, maintained by disparate individuals and companies and quickly become inconsistent and out of date.

items.summary: The IXP initiative - Building a path for newcomers
items.description: In DrupalCon Lille we officially started the IXP Initiative. The IXP Fellowship Initiative is a community-driven initiative to create incentives for companies to guide new developers through the very beginning of their career in Drupal. It seeks to address several issues:

items.summary: Working together: DDEV on Gitpod and GitHub Codespaces
items.description: Gitpod and GitHub Codespaces are great places to work on your Drupal/DDEV projects. (We don't be talking about Drupalpod here unless you want to, we'll have another session for Drupalpod, which is mostly for direct contributions to Drupal core and contrib.)

items.summary: Coffee Break 1, Tuesday
Enhancing TraceLink Website Experiences: Driving User Engagement and Business Growth through Digital Innovation (Sponsored by Material)
items.description: TraceLink has revolutionized the healthcare supply chain by creating the world’s largest digital network, which simplifies the global track-and-trace processes for pharmaceuticals.

items.summary: AI Meets Accessibility: Enhancing Drupal with Smart Image Descriptions (Sponsored by Hounder)
items.description: Hey there! I’m excited to talk to you about a cool new Drupal module I’ve been working on. You know how writing alt text for images can be a real pain, especially when you’ve got tons of pictures? Well, I’ve found a way to make it super easy using AI.

items.summary: Empowering content editors: managing content with ease in highly complex environments
items.description: Content management forms look complicated and are difficult to work with? You want to see your changes while doing them? Join my session and I will show you how to avoid stress and frustration, while empowering your content editor experience with realtime inline editing.

items.summary: Experience Builder Initiative
items.description: Join us to learn how we're planning to enable content creators and site builders to build fully customized pages and themes using no-code and low-code tools without ever leaving the browser.

items.summary: First-time contributor workshop 1
From Vision to Reality: Project Management in Multi-brand Platform with 140+ websites.
items.description: Ready to discover the magic behind managing 140+ websites in a multi-brand platform? Join us for an eye-opening session on turning visions into digital reality! Don't miss out on this captivating insight!

items.summary: How did we get 135,000 opinions and 67,000 points on maps from citizens about mobility, their city, quality of life, nature in 160 webform-projects? How do we create insights for decisions from all that data? What could this mean for your web solutio...
items.description: At Drupalcon Lille 2023, we showed our webform map solutions. Citizens from more than 100 cities and towns, including Brussels, Antwerp, Bilbao, Santander, Eindhoven, Edinburgh, Paris - collaborated on some 160 projects to improve their neighbourhood, city, region.

items.summary: How to use automated tools to upgrade your site from Drupal 7 to Drupal 10.x (Sponsored by Acquia)
items.description: 43% of the entire Drupal community is on Drupal 7. Drupal 7 end of life is coming in 2025. Acquia recently open sourced our migration automation tool, Acquia Migrate: Accelerate (AM:A) to help the community get to D10 and prepare for D11.

items.summary: Let's try to understand Typed Data, finally...
items.description: Typed Data API still remains quite a mystery for many. We need to get familiar with it in order to really know how entity data is modeled. In this session I'm going to get to the bottom of it, so please fasten your seatbelts and be ready to understand it. Finally...

items.summary: Migrations at scale: Migrating sites with millions of objects
items.description: We run Drupal migrations on every other project. The unique thing about our migrations is the amount of data we have. GLAM repositories sometimes have several million objects.

items.summary: Scaling Smiles: GC Dental's Mammoth Digital Transformation with Drupal
items.description: Embark on a professional journey into the heart of digital transformation as we unveil GC Dental's remarkable evolution of unification of brand identity and design standards across a multitude of brands, enhancing engagement and fostering trust among B2B customers, specifically dental professiona

items.summary: The PHP Foundation
items.description: I'm a founding board member of the PHP Foundation, ask me anything! The PHP Foundation is a collective of people and organizations relying on the PHP language. Its mission is to ensure the long-term prosperity of the PHP language. The PHP Foundation's mission statement:

items.summary: When Designers code - UX Paper Cuts at GitLab
items.description: We all know how painful a small cut on the tips of your finger can be. Not worth it for a doctor to check it, but painful enough to always remind you of its existence.

items.summary: AI with Drupal - Using LLM technology is easy, but how do you actually build useful applications?
items.description: AI functionalities based on Large Language Models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT from OpenAI are well integrated with Drupal. But finding the right use-cases for AI in custom projects can be quite challenging.

items.summary: Drupal Dives into Agile Waterfalls: How to Embrace Agile Principles in Traditional Project Environments
items.description: Have you ever tried to manage a closed scope project using agile methodologies, only to realize that the notorious "scope creep" had silently made its way into your meticulously planned backlog? Join us on this quest for flexible control, happy clients, and healthier teams.

items.summary: Drupal Gutenberg: Let's make it even better!
items.description: We'll present the new features of version 4 but mostly discuss the current state and the next steps for Drupal Gutenberg. Anyone can bring their own topics but here's some of them that we'd like to be discussed: - How Gutenberg will fit in Experience Builder.

items.summary: Engaging a user in a multibrand environment.
items.description: Seamlessly connect users from different brands spaces on a single multibrand website, coordinating identities and promoting engagement through a transversal loyalty experience.

items.summary: Innovation in action: Building a flexible and scalable Drupal platform for managing 60+ sites on Google Kubernetes Engine
items.description: In this session, we would like to share with you the secrets and strategies behind building a Drupal platform on Google Kubernetes Engine in our real example.

items.summary: Issue Triage
People's wisdom, crafting success one tip at a time
items.description: Everyone has his own little book of professional wisdom ("cada maestrillo tiene su librillo" we say in Spanish, which roughly translates to "each to their own way").

items.summary: Project Browser: making Starshot's in-browser feature composition a reality
items.description: Drupal Starshot puts Project Browser front and center, and will allow users to pick entire site templates and additional features during the installation process.

items.summary: The Evolution of Customer Interaction on a Pioneering Telecom Website in a Developing Country
items.description: Over a decade ago, a leading Telecom company embarked on a digital journey with the launch of its website using Drupal 6. Navigating the unique challenges posed by users in a developing country, the journey unfolded.

items.summary: The future of DrupalCamps - Hosting in-person events in the post-COVID era
items.description: The Drupal community is spread across the globe. Bringing the community together to foster collaboration and exchange, as well as to bring new people into the community, is a key driver of Drupal's success.

items.summary: Total Energies x Jakala: Achieving quality in large scale Drupal projects​: Retrospective on a Site Factory migration (Sponsored by Jakala)
items.description: Join us to explore the journey of TotalEnergies in migrating 200 websites from Drupal 7 to Drupal 10.

items.summary: Accelerating Transformation in Web Accessibility on Enterprise Level
items.description: We are all familiar with the concept of accessibility and its significance. In today's digital world, ensuring accessibility is not only a legal requirement but also a crucial element in creating inclusive and user-centered experiences for consumers.

items.summary: Catch 'em all: how to tackle major updates and refactors without releasing bugs to production.
items.description: Practical insights and best practices for Drupal major version at once for 35+ sites hosted on AWS.

items.summary: Drupal's Bundle Classes to Better Code
items.description: Bundle classes are PHP classes that define the structure and behavior of an entity bundle. Bundle classes in Drupal can help to separate logic and reduce coupling between modules by providing a centralized location for entity-related code.

items.summary: Jumpstart your Drupal contribution with Bug Smash Initiative
items.description: If you want to contribute to Drupal but are not sure where to start, or if you are frustrated by a core bug and want to see it fixed, join us in the Bug Smash Initiative! Prerequisite None but some familiarity with the Drupal core contribution process would be beneficial.

items.summary: Opening reception
Bridging the gap: Unlocking Non-Code Contributions
items.description: We all know about Non-code Contribution, but do we truly understand how it works? This panel discussion aims to shed light on what it involves, its challenges, and more, all from the perspective of contributors themselves.

items.summary: Deploy all your sites, all the ways, all the time. (Sponsored by amazee.io)
items.description: At amazee.io, we’ve encountered virtually every method of managing the deployment of multiple Drupal sites. Whether you’re running a few personal sites, managing a medium-sized agency, or overseeing a vast portfolio of brand sites, you’ll find valuable insights here.

items.summary: Fast Tracks to Compensation: Revolutionising Rail Claims with Drupal and AI - A Case Study
items.description: In a world where time is money, discover how we revolutionised the rail industry's Delay Repay claims management system using Drupal and AI, and reduced their processing time from 30 minutes to 5.

items.summary: Git on the Command Line - Office Hours
items.description: Do you want to improve your productivity and confidence with git? Let's work together to look at the basics of git on the command-line, and try to solve specific problems you may have. You can bring any project or concern or skill you want to work on. I'll share my preferred strategies.

items.summary: How I shoe-horned Drupal and PHP into Reykjavik University's Curriculum
items.description: Very few universities teach PHP as part of their curriculum, since it's not "fancy" enough. In 2020 I was able to shoehorn in a class, with a little bit of disguise, into the curriculum of Reykjavik University, where I'm now teaching it for the fifth time.

items.summary: Lessons from 12 years of mapping animals in and out of Drupal
items.description: Take a tip and bookmark packed dive through the vast landscape of interactive mapping sofware via tales of mapping seabirds, seals, sharks, trees, bears and more, and learn how far too many housecats almost put this developer off Drupal for life.

items.summary: Let’s burst our bubble: projects, products, estimates and forecasts.
items.description: Software estimation, despised by many, is one of the most contentious subjects in the world of software development. Constantly creating tension between developers, project managers, product managers, clients, budget holders and stakeholders.

items.summary: Membership Breakfast for Ripple Makers
The Admin UI Redesign Process
items.description: Curious about the design process behind the Drupal Admin UI redesign initiative? Join us as we walk you through the research, design and collaboration process while sharing concepts that didn't quite make the cut.

items.summary: The Circular Economy: Creating a Unique Search Experience to Facilitate an Environmentally Friendly Community
items.description: Love Essex is a site dedicated to dealing with waste in a responsible and environmentally friendly manner. Discover how the end user was at the very core of the creation process for this unique approach to functionality, education, and guidance.

items.summary: UI Suite DaisyUI Live Demo
items.description: UI Suite aims to improve Display building experience by integrating full Design systems (components, style utilities, layouts, examples, CSS variables, themes, icons sets, ...) in configuration everywhere in the Drupal UI.

items.summary: General Contribution Day 2
Coffee Break 1, Wednesday
Experts in Drupal (Sponsored by Hiberus)
items.description: We are one of the leading technology consulting companies with 100% private capital nationwide in Spain.

items.summary: How we improved service quality through innovation: Introducing SiteGuardian (Sponsored by CACI Digital Experience, Formely Cyber-Duck)
items.description: CACI Digital Experience (formerly Cyber-Duck) has been driving digital transformation for over 19 years, leveraging Drupal to deliver impactful solutions. Our clients include renowned organisations such as Sport England, the College of Policing, and the Bank of England.

items.summary: Coding beyond functionality. Altering technologies through artistic research.
items.description: In a moment when all conversations about technology seem to center on AI, machine learning, and the need for vast, unattainable resources, this keynote seeks to reclaim the importance of open, unproductive, unstable, and maker-culture-like practices.

items.summary: Breaking Boundaries: A Success Story of Crafting a Decoupled Web Portal for IDMC a part of the Norwegian Refugee Council (NRC)
items.description: The Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC) is the world’s leading data source and analysis on internal displacement.

items.summary: Building the future of Drupal: 11, 12 and Starshot
items.description: As of DrupalCon Barcelona 2024, Drupal 11.0 is still very new, but we already know when Drupal 12 will be released. Work on Starshot is well underway, and the Driesnote will provide a high-level update on its progress.

items.summary: Drupal Association Ripple Maker Roundtable
items.description: All Drupal Association individual members are invited to this round table discussion. The DA team will present our vision for the future of the membership program, and invite your questions and feedback. Experience levelBeginner

items.summary: Drupal for galleries, libraries, archives and museums (GLAM)
items.description: Let's exchange ideas on best practices for designing and developing Drupal digital repositories that cater to the unique needs of galleries, libraries, archives, and museums.

items.summary: First-time contributor workshop 1
From CMS to DXP: Bring your investment in Drupal to the next level (Sponsored by Dropsolid)
items.description: Now that everyone sees how important it is to build an open DXP to deliver personalised digital experiences to deliver ROI to customers in the form of double engagement and conversion rates, let's look at the fundamentals needed.

items.summary: Getting started with la_eu: local association site
items.description: The Dutch Drupal association shared their code for drupal.nl with the community. This resulted in the la_eu project. Multiple countries have launched their local site using this template and translated the english content to their language.

items.summary: Large-scale content creation with Drupal — Delights, Pitfalls and support structures to help editors
items.description: The new Website of a large public administration in Switzerland is based on Drupal. With a new structure and a setup built from scratch, the new website offers a state-of-the-art user experience. But to make this work, nearly all content had to be created from scratch.

items.summary: Reinventing the Digital Agency Business Model
items.description: The traditional digital agency business model is broken. By embracing an agile mindset and self-organization, we can reinvent our businesses in ways that increase engagement, reduce burnout, and improve outcomes.

items.summary: Web AccessibIlity and Site Governance with Drupal
items.description: How can we better build and maintain websites with accessibility built-in? This session hopes to explore best practices in accessibility-led website building and maintainance in Drupal.

items.summary: Webforms for everyone. Explore alternatives UIs for editors to create their own online forms
items.description: Webform is a great form builder but the complete interface can be too daunting for the typical Drupal editor Prerequisite Having played at least once with webform is enough to be able to get some insights from the session although some code will be presented.

items.summary: Designing for Privacy: Techniques and Methodologies for Balancing User Needs and Data Security
items.description: Hello! I'm Piyuesh, Director of Technology at QED42. Our team led a successful session at DrupalCon Lille, and I'm thrilled to share some advanced tips for creating secure digital experiences.

items.summary: Drupal AI: Once again leading the way with the new golden era of the web - The AI Module and Starshot
items.description: Time and time again Drupal has led the way in the golden era of the web and we continued to do so with AI.

items.summary: Drush - open discussion
items.description: Come with any topics you'd like to discuss. I'll come with some notes on the changes in Drush 13 in case folks want to hear about that.

items.summary: Exploring Agile Contracts: Maximizing Collaboration and Value in Client Relationships
items.description: Agile methodologies have revolutionized the way development projects are executed, emphasizing flexibility, collaboration, and value delivery. However, the traditional contract models often do not fully align with agile principles.

items.summary: Key Technical Decisions framework
items.description: In depth discussion about the Key Technical Decisions framework and process that was introduced in Lille 2023.

items.summary: Opening up Drupal's terminology
items.description: We want Drupal to be inclusive and accessible to everyone, and that starts with the words we use. The 'Drupalisms' issue was born from a belief that people shouldn't need to know how to ‘speak Drupal’ to be able to use it to create outstanding digital user experiences.

items.summary: Refactoring the DrupalCon Submission Review Process: A mini-retro with a focus to improve for next year
items.description: We will review the results of the DrupalCon Europe Submissions Survey, talk about some key takeaways and actions, and open the floor for community members to give their insights and experiences.

items.summary: Revisiting how to organise a Drupal event in your city or country: curiosities, tips and lessons learned
items.description: "Come for the code, stay for the community" – this is Drupal's motto, but...is it difficult to build community on your location, given the post-pandemic new reality, remote work, etc.? The answer is absolutely not! Prerequisite

items.summary: Third-Party Cookies Phase-out & Server-Side Tagging
items.description: Did you know that an average internet user is tracked by hundreds of trackers every day, often without their explicit consent? This invisible tracking has fueled the digital advertising industry but at the cost of privacy.

items.summary: Using AI to Speed Up Drupal Personalization (Sponsored by Pantheon)
items.description: Personalization has long been a "holy grail" for many web teams. Legendary in its power. But also hidden in parts unknown, out of reach. It takes a heroic journey to get there. Or does it?

items.summary: Helping nonprofits personalize Drupal websites with a CDP
items.description: While many nonprofits would like to add personalization to the website, most remain stuck at the starting line. We’ll walk through how we are piloting personalization for Boston Children's Hospital with no additional software costs using Lytics.

items.summary: Lunch Break, Wednesday
Progressive Migration Magic: Migrating Large Scale Drupal 7 Apps (Sponsored by Opensense Labs Pvt Ltd)
items.description: Discover how to navigate the complexities of migrating large-scale Drupal 7 applications. Our session will delve into real-world case studies, best practices, and expert insights to help you successfully transition to the latest Drupal versions.

items.summary: How not to be a Drupal Community’s Vampire? (Sponsored by NTT Data)
items.description: Commitment is an intrinsic part of NTT DATA’s corporate culture, and this of course applies to the Drupal community.

items.summary: Single Directory Components - a new web of possibilities (Sponsored by Hounder)
items.description: The Single Directory Component module (SDC), now integrated into Drupal core, offers indispensable benefits for effectively managing content organization and presentation on your website and directly support the Starshot initiative.

items.summary: Centarro Insights: Sales Analytics and Reports for Drupal Commerce (Sponsored by Centarro)
items.description: Drupal Commerce 2.37 added a dashboard for merchants to track their store's performance and receive updates from the project and module maintainers. It delivers useful summary statistics, but it is limited just to basic queries against the order tables in your store.

items.summary: How to set up cooperation with an AI vendor? (Sponsored by Lemberg Solutions)
items.description: Are you an agency considering extending your offering with AI services or a business looking to enhance your product using AI technologies? If any of the latter describes your case, you're in the right place.

items.summary: Building Safer Digital Communities - The Mission of "Defend Iceland"
items.description: In November 2023, Theódór Ragnar Gíslason, CTO and Head of Innovation at Syndis, an Icelandic cybersecurity firm, secured a €2.53M grant from the European Commission as part of the Digital Europe plan.

items.summary: Building strong virtual communities and digital ecosystems using Drupal: Transforming clients into happy engaged members of a flourishing ecosystem
items.description: Building bonded and loyal communities centered around common interests and needs is a pivotal strategy in marketing. We are going to delve into best practises and smart ideas of forming such vibrant communities and ecosystems.

items.summary: Developer Debugging Toolkit: Practical Techniques for Debugging Drupal Cache Problems
items.description: Unlock the mysteries of Drupal cache woes with our Developer Debugging Toolkit! Dive into practical techniques that'll empower you to conquer cache issues and turbocharge your site's performance.

items.summary: Drupal Association Public Board Meeting
items.description: Join us for a public board meeting of the Drupal Association. Board members will talk about future programs, conduct regular business to support the Drupal Association's non-profit operations, and and answer community questions. Experience levelBeginner
speakers: and the rest of the DA board

items.summary: DrupalCon’s Got Talent game show, test your skills and win prizes!
items.description: Fancy yourself as someone with mad website building skillz!? Think you have the creds to win the game show of the year? Then join the DrupalCon’s Got Talent’ (DGT) game show, the CivicTheme design system edition! Prerequisite Beginner Drupal content Management

items.summary: Get Paid and Famed for Core Accessibility Contributions
items.description: Sometimes working on a client project we run into accessibility issues that could be fixed in the core (or contrib). Let's always try to do just that. It's sometimes even easier - and you get credit for it, maybe even your client.

items.summary: How to Promote Drupal: The ultimate toolkit
items.description: Crafting your content involves more than just sharing a success story. In this session, we will share best practices involved in standing out among the noise, along with the ultimate toolkit for being a Drupal ambassador, covering how to approach:

items.summary: Kindness costs nothing: Leadership for a modern engineering organization
items.description: Leadership doesn't come from a title and having oodles of experience won't make you a great manager, so what does? Prerequisite None

items.summary: Leveraging the Command Pattern: Enhancing Drupal with Symfony Messenger
items.description: Managing user interactions can be complicated. Some operations must be executed immediately, whereas others can be scheduled for the future. Drupal core has batches and queues, but we can do better. We can dispatch commands that can be synchronous and asynchronous, in real-time or delayed.

items.summary: Mastering Enterprise Digital Solutions with Drupal: Unlocking the Power of Contrib Modules (Sponsored by Factorial GmbH)
items.description: In this session we'll explore how we used several key Contrib modules to create a complex, enterprise-level solution in one of our recent projects.

items.summary: Mentor orientation 1
Software Supply Chain Security Deep Dive
items.description: Join the maintainers of Drupal packaging infrastructure, Packagist and Rugged TUF Server for an in-depth discussion and Q&A about how the Drupal Association is helping to keep your Drupal sites up-to-date and secure.

items.summary: UI Patterns 2: Unleash SDC potential, plug your components directly in Drupal UI.
items.description: The UI Patterns community has large experience in building component-based Drupal solutions. We rethink UI Patterns to build a flexible and straightforward no-code solution on top of SDC to make it easy for site builders to bring the data to the components.

items.summary: Coffee Break 2, Wednesday
The Future of Digital Experience Platforms and AI Integration (Sponsored by QED42)
items.description: Piyuesh Kumar, Director of Technology at QED42, will explore the evolution of Digital Experience Platforms (DXPs) in addressing modern digital engagement needs and discuss the future of digital solutions with Space DXP integrated with AI technologies.

items.summary: DDEV Office Hours
items.description: Do you have questions about using DDEV, or a particular problem, or just want an introduction to using DDEV? Join us as we work together on anything you want to work on.

items.summary: Distracted during meetings? Try Sketchnoting
items.description: Distractions, whether internal or external, can significantly impede productivity and focus. Numerous studies have delved into understanding these distractions and devising strategies to mitigate their effects.

items.summary: Following Drupal core development: Is it possible to understand every added change?
items.description: Drupal moves faster than perceived. Keeping up to date is important and it may be challenging if you are not doing drupal core development full time. Let us explore Drupal core pace of changes, resources to follow it, and some experiences trying to do that.

items.summary: HTTP requests & more with kernel tests
items.description: Kernel tests are faster to run than browser tests, but they're typically only used for testing APIs.

items.summary: Mind & Body Stress Reduction Activities
Orange - Using Drupal to foster communities around external Digital Asset management system (Sponsored by Annertech)
items.description: Introduction

items.summary: Rules of development (and everything else for what matters)
items.description: Introduction to my black book of development rules, which I have compiled throughout my career in engineering and software development. We will use some of them as conversation starters so you can bring your own to the table.

items.summary: Strength through solidarity, resilience and collaboration: Overcoming challenges and productivity phenomenon of Ukrainian Drupal development teams during wartime
items.description: Embark on an inspiring journey into the resilience of Ukrainian Drupal development teams amid wartime adversity. Uncover how solidarity, resilience, and collaboration fuel productivity, offering invaluable lessons for teams facing challenges worldwide.

items.summary: Terms, Conditions, and Drupal: Spinning Legal Pages into Web Wonders
items.description: Welcome, fellow travellers, to a journey through the intricate pathways of legal compliance on the digital highway. Just as traffic laws regulate our conduct on the road, legal pages on your website serve as guiding lights, rules and regulations in the vast expanse of cyberspace.

items.summary: The final hurdle: Empowering and supporting your pitch team towards success
items.description: Not everyone is a natural salesperson, or wants to be. This session will center on how, in an increasingly competitive market, we can best support our pitch teams to win more new business and grow the global Drupal community.

items.summary: The Web APIs powering the Starshot trial experience
items.description: Join us to explore how this cutting-edge development could revolutionize the way users get started with Drupal, making it more accessible and convenient than ever before. Prerequisite Outline

items.summary: "Why on Earth Has This Not Been Released Yet?" - The Critical Gap in Drupal Contributions
items.description: "Why on Earth Has This Not Been Released Yet?" - The question, that often comes up in Drupal forums when a contributed code was tested by the community. In this session, we unveil the critical gap in tested and untested contributions and its consequences for the Drupal community.

items.summary: Building with Drupal in 2025: Reap the benefits of Drupal’s long-term architecture & modern features
items.description: Drupal's focus on a high-quality, maintainable architecture is paying off. Learn how even jr developers can efficiently build modern websites with Drupal in 2025. Prerequisite Knowledge of how websites are generally built using Drupal

items.summary: Drupal Staff Augmentation: Doing it right for Customer Delight
items.description: Digital projects extensively rely on Staff Augmentation for project capacity needs, and Staff Augmentation service providers have much within control to bring about customer delight.

items.summary: Gin Admin Theme - Road to stable
items.description: It's been a while since I've talked about the Gin Admin Theme! We've slowed down development quite a bit, but we've recently picked up speed again and cleared some of the big hurdles to finally get Gin to a stable release, even though it may feel like a meme now.

items.summary: Symfony Console: Evolving Your Drupal Toolkit with Symfony Console Commands
items.description: Drupal's evolution has been marked by the continual pursuit of developer experience improvements and usability improvement. Imagine how effortless it would be to create console commands in your Drupal application or module.

items.summary: General Contribution Day 3
Content/Comment-Analyzer 2.0
items.description: The City of Hamburg is pioneering an innovative approach to public engagement and content management through the integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) within their Drupal-based digital platforms.

items.summary: Drupal Certified Partners + Organization Members Roundtable
items.description: Engage with the Drupal Association's Development and Fundraising team.

items.summary: Drupal Recipes Initiative Update
items.description: "[The Recipes Initiative] is a great example of making it easy for people to be part of Drupal and the open web." - Dries Buytaert, Driesnote, DrupalCon Prague

items.summary: Drupal, the good parts
items.description: Websites are probably what Drupal does worst, but everything and everyone seems to be focused on that. I understand we are following the money, but I wonder what other things can be done with Drupal.

items.summary: Everything you need to know about cookies but are afraid to ask!
items.description: The world of cookies is changing. By the end of this year, Google aims to have phased out 3rd party cookies entirely for Chrome, which has big implications for website owners.

items.summary: How to come from stress to happiness - Wellness
Implementing AI solutions for the French government
items.description: As part of its strategic plan for this decade, the French government is pursuing its efforts to provide more and improved public services online.

items.summary: Open Source A11y Tools - How and Why?
items.description: Let's share experiences and learn from each other on checking for accessibility. Do you use Open Source Accessibility tools? Which one is your favourite? Is something missing from you toolbox? What are your regular workflows for checking issues?

items.summary: Prepare for Contribution Day: Issue Forks, Drupalpod, DDEV, Git
items.description: Let's get ready for Friday's contribution day. We'll refresh on * Issue Forks, what they are, how to use them * Using Drupalpod to test or fix an issue * DDEV skills and needs * Using git and how it relates to Drupalpod and Issue forks.

items.summary: Search API meets Typesense
items.description: Are you building a decoupled website or need advanced search capabilities like search-as-you-type and AI integration? Typesense may be the right solution for you; it's flexible, powerful, open source, and integrated with Drupal (using the Search API module).

items.summary: Unleash all Drupal features: how ambitious site builders take it to the next level with ECA and BPMN
items.description: The Events, Conditions, and Actions (ECA) module, also recognized by the Drupal Project Initiatives Keynote in Portland, revolutionizes Drupal's capabilities for site builders without having to write code or involve a programmer.

items.summary: Accessibility Beyond Compliance: Practical Insights for Drupal Users and Editors
items.description: In a world where accessibility compliance is more important than ever, it can be easy to fall into seeing this as a box ticking exercise.

items.summary: Coffee Break 1, Thursday
The Drupal Learning Management System (Sponsored by Systemseed Digital Services Llc)
items.description: Anu LMS is our open-source, Drupal-based, learning management system, currently used in over 70 countries. Join SystemSeed CTO, Evgeniy Maslovskiy, for a project update, to understand how you can use Anu, and learn about what's coming next.

items.summary: European Commission - EU Open Source Solutions catalogue - Demo (Sponsored by European Commission)
items.description: Join our session for a short demo of the EU Open Source Solutions Catalogue – an MVP developed by the European Commission on Drupal to showcase open source software created by public administrations across Europe.

items.summary: Drupal Business Survey 2024 Results BoF
items.description: The Drupal Business Survey investigate the trends in the digital market, in particular from service provides involved with the open source DXP Drupal. Digital agencies from all over the world participate in the yearly survey.

items.summary: Drupal LMS: A new, modern, easy-to-use Learning Management System
items.description: Education is the base of every career path, and too often it has a limited budget, but that does not mitigate its importance.

items.summary: Empowering Drupal for offline: Improved usability for low connectivity areas using PWA and IndexedDB
items.description: Explore the success story of Progressive Web Apps (PWA) and IndexedDB to enhance Drupal's capabilities and elevate user’s offline experience. Prerequisite Basic understanding of Drupal websites.

items.summary: Improved out-of-the-box experience with Sobki: demo of a design system oriented profile
items.description: The UI Suite ecosystem is composed of 5 main modules: UI Patterns UI Styles UI Skins UI Examples UI Icons (being developed during summer 2024)

items.summary: Local development environments for Drupal with DDEV
items.description: Learn to effectively use DDEV for local development of Drupal projects. This session will walk you through a wide array of features for professional Drupal development. Prerequisite No prior knowledge of DDEV or Docker is needed.

items.summary: Love thy CMS: Going from 0 to 60 with the Schema.org Blueprints module
items.description: A journey with a live demo showing how to go from 0 to 60 with the Schema.org Blueprints module. The session aims to help, inspire, and assist people to "Love thy CMS!"

items.summary: Mastering the Art of the Agency Pitch
items.description: As a communication agency, we help other agencies, technical product companies, and open source projects pitch themselves. But we have to pitch ourselves to them to get there!

items.summary: Mentor orientation 1
Quick and stable code delivery with Drupal in a Microservices environment (Sponsored by Platform.sh)
items.description: Snippet: Imagine you are tasked with integrating a legacy Drupal site into a microservices environment. How do you automate testing for stable code delivery? How do you set up your CI/CD pipelines Prerequisite: You use drupal and have a rough idea about APIs and microservices

items.summary: Svelte Javascript Framework in Drupal core via Project Browser
items.description: Come learn a bit about how we're using Svelte in Project Browser and identify how you can contribute to Drupal core while learning a modern, easy-to-understand JavaScript framework.

items.summary: Why a big deal async Drupal is
items.description: Discover how you can provide new experiences when moving beyond the synchronous world of web servers. Prerequisite This session requires no prior knowledge.

items.summary: Drupal Core Initiative Leads
items.description: Drupal’s initiatives form powerful teams that make great things happen. Core initiatives self-organize to get new improvements to the base system while community initiatives coordinate event organization, spread diversity best practices, collaborate on promotional materials, and so on.

items.summary: Embracing Open Source: The European Commission’s Journey with Drupal and the Interoperable Europe Portal (Sponsored by European Commission – DIGIT)
items.description: Learn how the European Commission is pioneering digital transformation through its Open Source Strategy, driving innovation and transparency across EU institutions.

items.summary: Lunch Break, Thursday
Sustainable websites & Business (Sponsored by Halvar.io)
items.description: This presentation is about what you as a web developer or website owner can do with your site but also with your company to get a lower footprint. With just a few small adjustments you can make a big difference.

items.summary: Turbocharging Membership Registration: Asynchronous Solutions with ReactPHP (Sponsored by CTI Digital)
items.description: In this technical deep dive, we will explore how we transformed a sluggish membership registration process into a swift and seamless experience.

items.summary: Next Generation of Drupal Developers: Effective Onboarding Strategies and Best Practices (Sponsored by Agiledrop)
items.description: The session will address the challenges of attracting developers to use PHP and sequentially recruiting them in companies and teams where Drupal is the framework of choice.

items.summary: Complex Drupal 7 Ubercart Store Migration to Drupal 10 (Sponsored by LN Webworks)
items.description: Little Background about Ubercart: Ubercart and Drupal commerce are two famous alternate solutions to get your ecommerce store settled up very quickly in Drupal. Ubercart has been heavily used for e-commerce setups by Drupal, but it's support has been deprecated after Drupal 7

items.summary: Being well at your desk (& beyond) - practical accessible bitesize activities to support your life-work balance.
items.description: We all know movement, exercise and screen breaks are recommended, do we do them? Probably not as much as we should! I’m sharing activities that can fit in your schedule at or near your desk! This is an interactive workshop offering practical modifiable takeaways.

items.summary: Configuration validation, how we got here and what is in the future
items.description: Configuration schema's power most of the configuration of your drupal sites, making those validatable paves the path towards easier config forms, recipies and decoupled admin ui's. Prerequisite

items.summary: Drupal + Modern JS without going headless
items.description: Sometimes headless is overkill! The Drupal Acceleration Team has found a way to make modern JS driven Drupal sites while keeping their head intact. Prerequisite Awareness of the pros/cons headless vs. non-headless sites.

items.summary: Drupal at the Heart of a Pharma's Education Revolution
items.description: Join us to explore pharma company’s transformative journey to a unified learning ecosystem with Drupal. From scattered sources to a centralized platform, discover how integrating AI, xAPI, and data analytics revolutionized training for global biopharmaceutical teams.

items.summary: Drupal.org Engineering Panel
items.description: Join the Drupal.org engineering team for updates on our work to support the community.

items.summary: Fractional vs. Full-Time Teams: Maximizing Efficiency and Expertise
items.description: Your talent needs probably fluctuate as you adapt and grow and the market changes around you. Fractional teams are on the rise at many companies in response to these changes in the professional landscape.

items.summary: Integration of AI in Drupal
items.description: Dive into the power of AI + Drupal. In this how we can achieve and use Power of Ai into our drupal websites.

items.summary: Local Drupal Regions & Associations Round Table
items.description: Local Drupal initiatives are at the heart of the Drupal community, bringing people together to exchange ideas, innovate, and celebrate the incredible things being done with Drupal every day.

items.summary: Recipes Initiative BoF
items.description: As a follow-up up to the Recipes Initiative Update, we can use this this time to discuss, real-life experiences building Recipes, potential opportunities for new recipes, some of the ways site builders can already use recipes, and more.

items.summary: Say Good-Bye to Your Staging Site: True Enterprise Content Management in Drupal With the Workspaces Module
items.description: Discover how to leverage the Workspaces module for implementing enterprise-ready publishing workflows by staging large sets of content changes!

items.summary: Where have the Drupal Hobbyists Gone? (And can Starshot bring them back?)
items.description: How many of us started out building Drupal sites as a hobby? Drupal used to be easy to download, install on low cost hosting, and build a site. Drupal was common for personal sites, blogs, photo galleries, forums, etc. But Drupal is rarely used for these any more.

items.summary: Drupal History in the European Commission
items.description: Drupal has a long history of usage in the European Commission (>10y at large scale) powering hundreds of websites from high profile communication websites to community websites where European citizens interact.

items.summary: 70 good reasons for a code refactoring
items.description: Discover how we upgraded 70 websites (50 countries + 20 local careers sites) to new branding, built a robust content delivery system, and followed country-specific requirements in a highly regulated environment.

items.summary: DCEU volunteer app
items.description: Have you been a volunteer this year? Please come and discuss your experiences using the new app. Any problems you've had, improvements that can be made, features to add? Let me know and we can sort them for next year.

items.summary: Drupal AI module and Starshot
items.description: Let's have a discussion about Drupal and AI! Learn about the features and sub modules of the AI abstraction layer and how we built the planned Starshot functionality. Learn how you can use it yourself or even contribute.

items.summary: Hidden Diamonds - The best lesser known Drupal modules you want to add to your website (Sponsored by 1xINTERNET)
items.description: Over the years we have built very many and very different websites. Some have many authenticated users, some integrate many APIs, some have many different editorial roles, some have a lot of content, some have highly structured content.

items.summary: Human Centred Design: An introduction for Drupal people (and how to make it work for you)
items.description: Human Centred Design is becoming more widely used as a UX and product design discipline. Join this session to learn what it is and how it can help you.

items.summary: Introducing the new Access Policy API in Drupal core
items.description: Want to make sure your employees do not work outside of office hours? Want to get rid of UID 1's all-access pass? Well now you can, thanks to the brand-new Access Policy API!

items.summary: Network of European Drupal Associations (NEDA)
items.description: In extension to the presentation of Joi Garret about the Local Associations Initiative during the BoF 'Local Drupal Regions & Associations Round Table', we come together to further discuss strategies for local associations and camps.

items.summary: Practical exploitation of Drupal security vulnerabilities
items.description: What would it look like if your site got hacked? What would the bad actors actually do, and why? How can you avoid this? Prerequisite No prerequisites.

items.summary: RAGify drupal - What drupal and a rag stack can do in the world of AI
items.description: This BoF will explore the possibilities of an integration of drupal into a RAG stack. First it will describe what a RAG stack generally is and what problems it can solve. Then it will discuss why it makes sense to integrate it with drupal , how that can be done and what advantages it gives.

items.summary: Securing the Modern Web: Strategies, Tools, and Ethical Hacking Techniques
items.description: Welcome to our workshop on "Securing the Modern Web: Strategies, Tools, and Ethical Hacking Techniques"! In today's digital landscape, web security is a critical concern. Join us as we delve into essential strategies, tools, and ethical hacking techniques designed to safeguard web applications.

items.summary: The Drupal API Client
items.description: The Drupal API Client project has made substantial progress since being selected for funding as part of the Pitch-burgh Innovation Contest. We're excited to share information about the 1.x release of our JSON:API Client, and what the future holds for this initiative.

items.summary: Coffee Break 2, Thursday
blökkli - Demo, Q&A for the Interactive page building experience with Nuxt.
items.description: Join us to see https://blokk.li/ in action, get your questions answered and find out how-to contribute & what are the first steps to try it out.

items.summary: Consolidating core development tools
items.description: This is a session to discuss the current landscape for core and contrib local development, and plan a roadmap. Newcomers to core and contrib contribution are easily baffled by the wide range of tools and techniques for local development.

items.summary: Drupal on Cloud Nine: Maximizing GitHub CodeSpaces for Development and Beyond
items.description: Explore the Future of Drupal Development: Leverage GitHub CodeSpaces for efficient, containerised Drupal site development and testing - completely free.

items.summary: Empowering Drupal developers: redefining training and mentorship
items.description: In this session, I'll share a story of several years on developing company's mentoring and training programs. In the beginning, they lacked organization and a coherent structure. I brought there my experience as a mentor and my love for Drupal.

items.summary: Empowering QA as Defenders of Business Goals and Client Satisfaction
items.description: QA bridging the gap between what the business needs and what the dev team does. Ensuring the best implementation of the best product for a satisfied customer.

items.summary: Future of the Gin admin theme
items.description: Join the lead maintainer of Gin Sascha Eggenberger and Starshot UX lead Cristina Chumillas to discuss the future of Gin and get involved. (Note for content admin: make Sascha and Cristina the owners of this BoF is possible on the schedule and omit Gábor :)

items.summary: Lazy loading content for better performance, UX and SEO
items.description: Improve your application look & feel and performance using Drupal's build-in capabilities. Prerequisite Medium to advanced knowledge of Drupal front-end development.

items.summary: Let‘s talk headless Drupal
items.description: This BoF session invites everyone to share their experiences with Drupal and headless architecture, discuss challenges, and brainstorm ways to improve headless Drupal solutions. Whether you're a frontend developer, backend expert, or content manager, your input is valuable.

items.summary: Migrating the A11yTalks Website from React Components to Single Directory Components
items.description: Learn how the A11yTalks website was moved from Gatsby to Drupal using Single Directory Components. Prerequisite Attendees will need familiarity with Drupal and an interest in Single Directory Components

items.summary: Supply Chain Security in Drupal and Composer
items.description: In the modern world of 'software built elsewhere' and complex dependency trees, supply chain security is more important than ever.

items.summary: Ahead of the Curve: The Power of Shift Left in Ensuring Quality
items.description: Dive into the 'Shift Left' revolution where early testing meets efficiency, transforming the DNA of software development and quality assurance for the agile era.

items.summary: Automated or near-automated migrations from Drupal 7 to 10
items.description: We are going to show you how we have managed to reduce the time needed to migrate a site from Drupal 7 to Drupal 10 to minutes. Prerequisite None

items.summary: How we convinced our boss to create a team of makers
items.description: This is a real life story of how we turned a team of takers in to a team of makers by convincing the company to allow the whole Drupal team to spend a day every other week innovating, working together to do Drupal better and make Drupal better.

items.summary: The Goldilocks principle in drupal projects - It is all about being "just right"!
items.description: Customer Value, Team Empowerment and Profitability - The balancing act while delivering Drupal projects from Goldilocks & the Three Bears!

items.summary: Wrap Up Ceremony Auditorium - DrupalCon 2025 city Announcement
Trivia Night
Contribution Day
First Time Contributor Workshop 1
Mentored Contribution
Coffe Break 1, Friday
Lunch Break, Friday
Coffee Break 2, Friday
